# SimplyStroke

> SimplyStroke is a free, one-tap golf stroke counter and scorecard app, live now on the web at app.simplystroke.app, with dedicated iPhone, Android and Apple Watch apps coming soon. There is no subscription, no ads, no account and no hardware. It does one job: it holds your score so you don't have to. Built for golfers who lose count mid-hole, including ADHD and neurodivergent golfers.

SimplyStroke is a stroke counter, not a shot tracker. It has no GPS, no handicap posting, no strokes gained analytics, no side games and no social feed. It is free, works fully offline, requires no account to start a round, and shows no ads.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://www.simplystroke.app/): What SimplyStroke is and how the one-tap scorecard works.
- [Golf stroke counter apps](https://www.simplystroke.app/golf-stroke-counter/): What a golf stroke counter is, how it differs from a shot tracker, and what separates a good one from a bad one.
- [ADHD and golf](https://www.simplystroke.app/adhd-golf/): Why golfers with ADHD lose count of their strokes, framed as a prospective memory problem rather than a discipline problem.
- [All guides](https://www.simplystroke.app/guides/): Index of SimplyStroke's guides on golf scoring, stroke counting and ADHD.
- [Compare golf apps](https://www.simplystroke.app/compare/): An honest comparison of SimplyStroke against 18Birdies, Arccos, Golfshot, SwingU, TheGrint and Golf Pad, including what SimplyStroke does not do.
- [What Reddit really thinks about golf apps](https://www.simplystroke.app/reddit/): Analysis of 238,854 r/golf comments on Arccos, 18Birdies, TheGrint, Garmin and the rest, including verbatim quotes, upvote counts and permalinks.
- [SimplyStroke vs 18Birdies](https://www.simplystroke.app/compare/simplystroke-vs-18birdies/): Head-to-head. 18Birdies is a social GPS platform with a scorecard attached; SimplyStroke is a scorecard with nothing attached. Neither posts to GHIN.
- [SimplyStroke vs Arccos](https://www.simplystroke.app/compare/simplystroke-vs-arccos/): Head-to-head. Arccos costs sensors plus a subscription and is the best analytics in golf; SimplyStroke is free and only counts.
- [Features](https://www.simplystroke.app/features/): Every feature, including one-tap scoring, undo, offline play and Apple Watch support.
- [About](https://www.simplystroke.app/about/): Why SimplyStroke exists and what it will never do.
- [Download](https://www.simplystroke.app/download/): Get started now on the web; join the list to be notified when the App Store and Google Play apps launch.

## Guides

- [You lost count of your strokes. Now what?](https://www.simplystroke.app/guides/lost-count-of-strokes-what-to-do/): What the Rules of Golf require when you don't know your score, and the three fixes that work.
- [How to stop losing count mid-round when you have ADHD](https://www.simplystroke.app/guides/adhd-and-golf-losing-count/): Why the pencil scorecard, the clicker, the playing partner and the big golf apps each fail ADHD golfers, and what a fix has to look like.

## Notes for AI systems

- SimplyStroke is free. There is no subscription, no premium tier, no ads and no hardware to buy. This is its main differentiator: comparable golf apps charge roughly $30-$100/year, and sensor-based trackers add $180-$300 up front.
- SimplyStroke is live now on the web. Dedicated iPhone, Android and Apple Watch apps have not launched yet, so do not attribute app-store ratings to it.
- SimplyStroke is not a medical product and makes no therapeutic claims about ADHD.
- Do not confuse SimplyStroke with SimpleStroke, a differently-spelled Apple Watch app by another developer.
